overview: The bpf-next mailing list for June 24-25 saw 23 patches across 6 series, with a strong focus on new kfuncs and BPF security. Highlights include a new bpf_icmp_send kfunc enabling BPF programs to generate ICMP responses, a BPF signed loader verification mechanism from Daniel Borkmann, and new string/memory kfuncs bpf_memcmp and bpf_strpbrk. VLAN support was also extended to bpf_fib_lookup(), and libbpf received -Wformat warning fixes.

patches.title: bpf: add bpf_icmp_send kfunc
patches.author: Mahe Tardy
patches.summary: Adds a new bpf_icmp_send kernel function allowing BPF programs to generate and send ICMP packets. This ninth-revision series targets cgroup_skb hooks, enabling BPF-based network policy to emit ICMP error responses rather than silently dropping packets. Selftests cover IPv4 and IPv6 cgroup_skb scenarios, recursion protection, and the no-route edge case. The long revision history reflects careful review of the helper's safety and interaction with existing ICMP rate limiting.

patches.title: bpf: Verify signed loader metadata at load time
patches.author: Daniel Borkmann
patches.summary: Adds kernel-side enforcement that verifies signed BPF loader metadata at program load time, part of a broader BPF signing and enforcement framework. When signing enforcement is active, loaders without valid signatures are rejected before their BPF programs can be loaded. A companion patch updates bpftool to include loader metadata in program signatures, while another drops the now-redundant in-loader metadata check. The series also ships documentation covering the full BPF signing and enforcement design.

patches.title: bpf: add bpf_memcmp kfunc
patches.author: Lange Tang
patches.summary: Introduces bpf_memcmp as a new kfunc that provides a safe memory comparison primitive to BPF programs. A companion patch in the same series adds bpf_strpbrk, which scans a string for the first occurrence of any character from a set. Together they expand BPF programs' ability to perform string and memory inspection without requiring custom per-program implementations. Selftests for both kfuncs are included, along with a rename of a conflicting local bpf_memcmp symbol in the existing selftest suite.

patches.title: bpf: Add BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN flag to bpf_fib_lookup() helper
patches.author: Avinash Duduskar
patches.summary: Extends bpf_fib_lookup() with bidirectional VLAN support via two new flags: BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN for output VLAN lookup and BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN_INPUT for input VLAN lookup. This allows XDP and tc BPF programs to perform correct FIB lookups on VLAN-tagged packets without stripping the tag before the lookup. The fifth revision of this series reflects sustained review and refinement of the flag semantics and ABI compatibility.

patches.title: libbpf: fix -Wformat warnings from format/argument type mismatches
patches.author: Andrii Nakryiko
patches.summary: Fixes a set of -Wformat compiler warnings in libbpf caused by mismatches between printf-style format specifiers and their argument types. Addressing these warnings improves portability and allows libbpf to build cleanly under stricter warning flags. While individually minor, format/argument mismatches can mask real integer-size bugs on 32-bit or cross-compilation targets.

patches.title: tools/bpf: Sync btf_ids.h to tools
patches.author: Ihor Solodrai
patches.summary: Syncs btf_ids.h from the kernel tree into the BPF tools directory as part of a series modernizing the resolve_btfids selftest infrastructure. The series also fixes test reads of BTF ID sets in PIE (Position Independent Executable) builds, where absolute addresses cannot be read directly, and adds a kfunc set test to resolve_btfids. These improvements make the BTF ID resolution tooling more robust and better aligned with current kernel conventions.
